heading: Rules of origin
label: 4531 Rules of origin

children.heading: Aquaculture
children.label: 1 Aquaculture
children.text: The term “aquaculture” means the farming of aquatic organisms, including fish, molluscs, crustaceans, other aquatic invertebrates, and aquatic plants from seed stock such as eggs, fry, fingerlings, or larvae, by intervention in the rearing or growth processes to enhance production such as regular stocking, feeding, or protection from predators.

children.heading: Customs Valuation Agreement
children.label: 2 Customs Valuation Agreement
children.text: The term “Customs Valuation Agreement” means the Agreement on Implementation of Article VII of the General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade 1994 referred to in .section 3511(d)(8) of this title

children.heading: Fungible good or fungible material
children.label: 3 Fungible good or fungible material
children.text: The term “fungible good” or “fungible material” means a good or material, as the case may be, that is interchangeable with another good or material for commercial purposes and the properties of which are essentially identical to such other good or material.

children.heading: Good wholly obtained or produced entirely in the territory of one or more USMCA countries
children.label: 4 Good wholly obtained or produced entirely in the territory of one or more USMCA countries
A
children.text: A mineral good or other naturally occurring substance extracted or taken from the territory of one or more USMCA countries.

children.label: B
children.text: A plant, plant good, vegetable, or fungus grown, cultivated, harvested, picked, or gathered in the territory of one or more USMCA countries.

children.label: C
children.text: A live animal born and raised in the territory of one or more USMCA countries.

children.label: D
children.text: A good obtained in the territory of one or more USMCA countries from a live animal.

children.label: E
children.text: An animal obtained by hunting, trapping, fishing, gathering, or capturing in the territory of one or more USMCA countries.

children.label: F
children.text: A good obtained in the territory of one or more USMCA countries from aquaculture.

children.label: i
children.text: a vessel that is registered or recorded with a USMCA country and flying the flag of that country; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a vessel that is documented under the laws of the United States.

children.label: i
children.text: is registered or recorded with a USMCA country and flies the flag of that country; or

children.label: ii
children.text: is a vessel that is documented under the laws of the United States.

children.label: I
children.text: A good, other than a good referred to in subparagraph (G), that is taken by a USMCA country, or a person of a USMCA country, from the seabed or subsoil outside the territory of a USMCA country, if that USMCA country has the right to exploit such seabed or subsoil.

children.label: i
children.text: production in the territory of one or more USMCA countries; or

children.label: ii
children.text: used goods collected in the territory of one or more USMCA countries, if such goods are fit only for the recovery of raw materials.

children.label: K
children.text: A good produced in the territory of one or more USMCA countries exclusively from goods referred to in any of subparagraphs (A) through (J), or from their derivatives, at any stage of production.

children.label: A
children.text: fuel and energy;

children.label: B
children.text: tools, dies, and molds;

children.label: C
children.text: spare parts and materials used or consumed in the maintenance of equipment or buildings;

children.label: D
children.text: lubricants, greases, compounding materials, and other materials used or consumed in production or to operate equipment or buildings;

children.label: E
children.text: gloves, glasses, footwear, clothing, safety equipment, and supplies;

children.label: F
children.text: equipment, devices, and supplies used for testing or inspecting the good;

children.label: G
children.text: catalysts and solvents; and

children.label: H
children.text: any other material that is not incorporated into the good, if the use of the material in the production of the good can reasonably be demonstrated to be a part of that production.

children.heading: Intermediate material
children.label: 6 Intermediate material
children.text: The term “intermediate material” means a material that is self-produced, used or consumed in the production of a good, and designated as an intermediate material pursuant to subsection (d)(9).

children.heading: Material
children.label: 7 Material
children.text: The term “material” means a good that is used or consumed in the production of another good and includes a part or an ingredient.

children.heading: Net cost
children.label: 8 Net cost
children.text: The term “net cost” means total cost minus sales promotion, marketing, and after-sales service costs, royalties, shipping and packing costs, and nonallowable interest costs that are included in the total cost.

children.heading: Net cost of a good
children.label: 9 Net cost of a good
children.text: The term “net cost of a good” means the net cost that can be reasonably allocated to a good using one of the methods set forth in subsection (d)(7).

children.heading: Nonallowable interest costs
children.label: 10 Nonallowable interest costs
children.text: The term “nonallowable interest costs” means interest costs incurred by a producer that exceed 700 basis points above the applicable official interest rate for comparable maturities of the country in which the producer is located.

children.heading: Nonoriginating good or nonoriginating material
children.label: 11 Nonoriginating good or nonoriginating material
children.text: The term “nonoriginating good” or “nonoriginating material” means a good or material, as the case may be, that does not qualify as originating under this section.

children.heading: Originating good; originating material
children.label: 12 Originating good; originating material
children.text: The term “originating good” or “originating material” means a good or material, as the case may be, that qualifies as originating under this section.

children.heading: Packaging materials and containers
children.label: 13 Packaging materials and containers
children.text: The term “packaging materials and containers” means materials and containers in which a good is packaged for retail sale.

children.heading: Packing materials and containers
children.label: 14 Packing materials and containers
children.text: The term “packing materials and containers” means materials and containers that are used to protect a good during transportation.

children.heading: Producer
children.label: 15 Producer
children.text: The term “producer” means a person who engages in the production of a good.

children.label: A
children.text: growing, cultivating, raising, mining, harvesting, fishing, trapping, hunting, capturing, breeding, extracting, manufacturing, processing, or assembling a good; or

children.label: B
children.text: the farming of aquatic organisms through aquaculture.

children.heading: Reasonably allocate
children.label: 17 Reasonably allocate
children.text: The term “reasonably allocate” means to apportion in a manner appropriate to the circumstances.

children.label: A
children.text: the disassembly of a used good into individual parts; and

children.label: B
children.text: the cleaning, inspecting, testing, or other processing that is necessary for improvement to sound working condition of such individual parts.

children.label: A
children.text: is entirely or partially composed of recovered materials;

children.label: B
children.text: has a life expectancy similar to, and performs in a manner that is the same as or similar to, such a good when new; and

children.label: C
children.text: has a factory warranty similar to that applicable to such a good when new.

children.label: A
children.text: personnel training, without regard to where the training is performed; or

children.label: B
children.text: if performed in the territory of one or more USMCA countries, engineering, tooling, die-setting, software design and similar computer services, or other services.

children.heading: Sales promotion, marketing, and after-sales service costs
children.label: 21 Sales promotion, marketing, and after-sales service costs
A
children.text: Sales and marketing promotion, media advertising, advertising and market research, promotional and demonstration materials, exhibits, sales conferences, trade shows, conventions, banners, marketing displays, free samples, sales, marketing, and after-sales service literature (product brochures, catalogs, technical literature, price lists, service manuals, and sales aid information), establishment and protection of logos and trademarks, sponsorships, wholesale and retail charges, and entertainment.

children.label: B
children.text: Sales and marketing incentives, consumer, retailer, or wholesaler rebates, and merchandise incentives.

children.label: C
children.text: Salaries and wages, sales commissions, bonuses, benefits (such as medical, insurance, and pension benefits), traveling and living expenses, and membership and professional fees for sales promotion, marketing, and after-sales service personnel.

children.label: D
children.text: Product liability insurance.

children.label: E
children.text: Rent and depreciation of sales promotion, marketing, and after-sales service offices and distribution centers.

children.label: F
children.text: Payments by the producer to other persons for warranty repairs.

children.label: i
children.text: Property insurance premiums, taxes, utilities, and repair and maintenance of sales promotion, marketing, and after-sales service offices and distribution centers.

children.label: ii
children.text: Recruiting and training of sales promotion, marketing, and after-sales service personnel, and after-sales training of customers’ employees.

children.label: iii
children.text: Office supplies for sales promotion, marketing, and after-sales service of goods.

children.label: iv
children.text: Telephone, mail, and other communications.

children.heading: Self-produced material
children.label: 22 Self-produced material
children.text: The term “self-produced material” means a material that is produced by the producer of a good and used in the production of that good.

children.heading: Shipping and packing costs
children.label: 23 Shipping and packing costs
children.text: The term “shipping and packing costs” means the costs incurred in packing a good for shipment and shipping the good from the point of direct shipment to the buyer, excluding the costs of preparing and packaging the good for retail sale.

children.heading: Territory
children.label: 24 Territory
children.text: The term “territory”, with respect to a USMCA country, has the meaning given that term in section C of chapter 1 of the USMCA.

children.label: i
children.text: means all product costs, period costs, and other costs for a good incurred in the territory of one or more USMCA countries; and

children.label: I
children.text: profits that are earned by the producer of the good, regardless of whether the costs are retained by the producer or paid out to other persons as dividends; or

children.label: II
children.text: taxes paid on those profits, including capital gains taxes.

children.heading: Other costs
children.label: i Other costs
children.text: The term “other costs” means all costs recorded on the books of the producer that are not product costs or period costs, such as interest.

children.heading: Period costs
children.label: ii Period costs
children.text: The term “period costs” means costs, other than product costs, that are expensed in the period in which they are incurred, such as selling expenses and general and administrative expenses.

children.heading: Product costs
children.label: iii Product costs
children.text: The term “product costs” means costs that are associated with the production of a good, including the value of materials, direct labor costs, and direct overhead.

children.label: A
children.text: actually paid or payable for a good or material with respect to a transaction of a producer; and

children.label: B
children.text: adjusted in accordance with the principles set forth in paragraphs 1, 3, and 4 of article 8 of the Customs Valuation Agreement.

children.heading: USMCA country
children.label: 27 USMCA country
children.text: The term “USMCA country” means the United States, Canada, or Mexico for such time as the USMCA is in force with respect to Canada or Mexico, and the United States applies the USMCA to Canada or Mexico.

children.heading: Value
children.label: 28 Value
children.text: The term “value” means the value of a good or material for purposes of calculating customs duties or applying this section.

children.heading: Tariff classification
children.label: 1 Tariff classification
children.text: The basis for any tariff classification is the HTS.

children.heading: Reference to HTS
children.label: 2 Reference to HTS
children.text: Whenever in this section there is a reference to a chapter, heading, or subheading, that reference shall be a reference to a chapter, heading, or subheading of the HTS.

children.heading: Cost or value
children.label: 3 Cost or value
children.text: Any cost or value referred to in this section with respect to a good shall be recorded and maintained in accordance with the generally accepted accounting principles applicable in the territory of the USMCA country in which the good is produced.

children.label: A
children.text: the good is a good wholly obtained or produced entirely in the territory of one or more USMCA countries;

children.label: B
children.text: the good is produced entirely in the territory of one or more USMCA countries using nonoriginating materials, if the good satisfies all applicable requirements set forth in Annex 4–B of the USMCA; or

children.label: C
children.text: the good is produced entirely in the territory of one or more USMCA countries, exclusively from originating materials;

children.label: i
children.text: the good is produced entirely in the territory of one or more USMCA countries;

children.label: I
children.text: both the good and its materials are classified under the same subheading or under the same heading that is not further subdivided into subheadings; or

children.label: II
children.text: the good was imported into the territory of a USMCA country in an unassembled form or a disassembled form but was classified as an assembled good pursuant to rule 2(a) of the General Rules of Interpretation of the HTS; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the regional value content of the good is not less than 60 percent if the transaction value method is used, or not less than 50 percent if the net cost method is used and the good satisfies all other applicable requirements of this section; or

children.label: E
children.text: the good itself, as imported, is listed in table 2.10.1 of the USMCA and is imported into the territory of the United States from the territory of a USMCA country.

children.heading: Remanufactured goods
children.label: 2 Remanufactured goods
children.text: For purposes of determining whether a remanufactured good is an originating good, a recovered material derived in the territory of one or more USMCA countries shall be treated as originating if the recovered material is used or consumed in the production of, and incorporated into, the remanufactured good.

children.heading: Special rule for foreign-trade zones
children.label: 3 Special rule for foreign-trade zones
children.text: Paragraph (1)(B) shall not apply to a good produced in a foreign-trade zone or subzone established pursuant to the Act of (commonly known as the “Foreign Trade Zones Act”) ( et seq.) that is entered for consumption in the customs territory of the United States.June 18, 193419 U.S.C. 81a

children.label: A
children.text: the transaction value method described in paragraph (2); or

children.label: B
children.text: the net cost method described in paragraph (3).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An importer, exporter, or producer of a good may calculate the regional value content of the good on the basis of the following transaction value method:rvc=tv–vnm×100tv

children.heading: RVC
children.label: i RVC
children.text: The term “RVC” means the regional value content of the good, expressed as a percentage.

children.heading: TV
children.label: ii TV
children.text: The term “TV” means the transaction value of the good, adjusted to exclude any costs incurred in the international shipment of the good.

children.heading: VNM
children.label: iii VNM
children.text: The term “VNM” means the value of nonoriginating materials used by the producer in the production of the good.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An importer, exporter, or producer of a good may calculate the regional value content of the good on the basis of the following net cost method:rvc=nc–vnm×100nc

children.heading: NC
children.label: i NC
children.text: The term “NC” means the net cost of the good.

children.heading: RVC
children.label: ii RVC
children.text: The term “RVC” means the regional value content of the good, expressed as a percentage.

children.heading: VNM
children.label: iii VNM
children.text: The term “VNM” means the value of nonoriginating materials used by the producer in the production of the good.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The value of nonoriginating materials used by the producer in the production of a good shall not, for purposes of calculating the regional value content of the good under paragraph (2) or (3), include the value of nonoriginating materials used or consumed to produce originating materials that are subsequently used or consumed in the production of the good.

children.heading: Special rule for certain components
children.label: B Special rule for certain components
i
children.text: The value of processing the nonoriginating materials undertaken in the territory of one or more USMCA countries.

children.label: ii
children.text: The value of any originating materials used or consumed in the production of the nonoriginating materials undertaken in the territory of one or more USMCA countries.

children.heading: Net cost method required in certain cases
children.label: 5 Net cost method required in certain cases
children.text: An importer, exporter, or producer of a good shall calculate the regional value content of the good solely on the basis of the net cost method described in paragraph (3) if the rule for the good set forth in Annex 4–B of the USMCA includes a regional value content requirement not based on the transaction value method described in paragraph (2).

children.heading: Net cost method allowed for adjustments
children.label: 6 Net cost method allowed for adjustments

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If an importer, exporter, or producer of a good calculates the regional value content of the good on the basis of the transaction value method described in paragraph (2) and a USMCA country subsequently notifies the importer, exporter, or producer, during the course of a verification conducted in accordance with chapter 5 or 6 of the USMCA, that the transaction value of the good or the value of any material used in the production of the good must be adjusted or is unacceptable under article 1 of the Customs Valuation Agreement, the importer, exporter, or producer may calculate the regional value content of the good on the basis of the net cost method.

children.label: i
children.text: the transaction value of a good; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the value of any material used in the production of a good.

children.label: A
children.text: calculating the total cost incurred with respect to all goods produced by that producer, subtracting any sales promotion, marketing, and after-sales services costs, royalties, shipping and packing costs, and nonallowable interest costs that are included in the total cost of those goods, and then reasonably allocating the resulting net cost of those goods to the good;

children.label: B
children.text: calculating the total cost incurred with respect to all goods produced by that producer, reasonably allocating the total cost to the good, and subtracting any sales promotion, marketing, and after-sales service costs, royalties, shipping and packing costs, and nonallowable interest costs, that are included in the portion of the total cost allocated to the good; or

children.label: C
children.text: reasonably allocating each cost that is part of the total cost incurred with respect to the good so that the aggregate of those costs does not include any sales promotion, marketing, and after-sales service costs, royalties, shipping and packing costs, and nonallowable interest costs.

children.heading: Value of materials used in production
children.label: 8 Value of materials used in production
A
children.text: in the case of a material that is imported by the producer of the good, the transaction value of the material at the time of importation, including the costs incurred in the international shipment of the material;

children.label: i
children.text: the price paid or payable by the producer in the USMCA country where the producer is located;

children.label: ii
children.text: the value as determined under subparagraph (A), as set forth in regulations prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury providing for the application of transaction value in the absence of an importation by the producer; or

children.label: iii
children.text: the earliest ascertainable price paid or payable in the territory of the country; or

children.label: i
children.text: all expenses incurred in the production of the material, including general expenses; and

children.label: ii
children.text: an amount for profit equivalent to the profit added in the normal course of trade or equal to the profit that is usually reflected in the sale of goods of the same class or kind as the material.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Any self-produced material that is used in the production of a good may be designated by the producer of the good as an intermediate material for purposes of calculating the regional value content of the good under paragraph (2) or (3).

children.heading: Materials used in production of intermediate materials
children.label: B Materials used in production of intermediate materials
children.text: If a self-produced material is designated as an intermediate material under subparagraph (A) for purposes of calculating a regional value content requirement, no other self-produced material subject to a regional value content requirement used or consumed in the production of that intermediate material may be designated by the producer as an intermediate material.

children.heading: Further adjustments to value of materials
children.label: 10 Further adjustments to value of materials
A
children.text: The costs of freight, insurance, packing, and all other costs incurred in transporting the material to the location of the producer.

children.label: B
children.text: Duties, taxes, and customs brokerage fees on the material paid in the territory of one or more USMCA countries, other than duties or taxes that are waived, refunded, refundable, or otherwise recoverable, including credit against duty or tax paid or payable.

children.label: C
children.text: The cost of waste and spoilage resulting from the use of the material in the production of the good, less the value of renewable scrap or byproducts.

children.heading: Producers
children.label: 1 Producers
children.text: A good that is produced in the territory of one or more USMCA countries, by one or more producers, is an originating good if the good satisfies the requirements of subsection (c) and all other applicable requirements of this section.

children.heading: Originating materials used in production of goods of a USMCA country
children.label: 2 Originating materials used in production of goods of a USMCA country
children.text: Originating materials from the territory of one or more USMCA countries that are used in the production of a good in the territory of another USMCA country shall be considered to originate in the territory of such other USMCA country.

children.heading: Production undertaken on nonoriginating materials used in the production of goods
children.label: 3 Production undertaken on nonoriginating materials used in the production of goods
children.text: In determining whether a good is an originating good under this section, production undertaken on nonoriginating material in the territory of one or more USMCA countries by one or more producers shall contribute to the originating status of the good, regardless of whether that production is sufficient to confer originating status to the nonoriginating material.

children.heading: De minimis amounts of nonoriginating materials
children.label: f De minimis amounts of nonoriginating materials
i
children.text: does not exceed 10 percent of the transaction value of the good, adjusted to exclude any costs incurred in the international shipment of the good; or

children.label: ii
children.text: does not exceed 10 percent of the total cost of the good;

children.label: B
children.text: the good meets all other applicable requirements of this section; and

children.label: C
children.text: the value of such nonoriginating materials is included in the value of nonoriginating materials for any applicable regional value content requirement for the good.

children.heading: Exceptions for dairy and other products
children.label: 2 Exceptions for dairy and other products
A
children.text: A nonoriginating material of headings 0401 through 0406, or a nonoriginating dairy preparation containing over 10 percent by dry weight of milk solids of subheading 1901.90 or 2106.90, used or consumed in the production of a good of headings 0401 through 0406.

children.label: i
children.text: Infant preparations containing over 10 percent by dry weight of milk solids, of subheading 1901.10.

children.label: ii
children.text: Mixes and doughs containing over 25 percent by dry weight of butterfat, not put up for retail sale, of subheading 1901.20.

children.label: iii
children.text: A dairy preparation containing over 10 percent by dry weight of milk solids, of subheading 1901.90 or 2106.90.

children.label: iv
children.text: A good of heading 2105.

children.label: v
children.text: Beverages containing milk of subheading 2202.90.

children.label: vi
children.text: Animal feeds containing over 10 percent by dry weight of milk solids of subheading 2309.90.

children.label: C
children.text: A nonoriginating material of heading 0805, or any of subheadings 2009.11 through 2009.39, used or consumed in the production of a good of subheadings 2009.11 through 2009.39, or a fruit or vegetable juice of any single fruit or vegetable, fortified with minerals or vitamins, concentrated or unconcentrated, of subheading 2106.90 or 2202.90.

children.label: D
children.text: A nonoriginating material of chapter 9 used or consumed in the production of instant coffee, not flavored, of subheading 2101.11.

children.label: E
children.text: A nonoriginating material of chapter 15 used or consumed in the production of a good of any of headings 1501 through 1508 1512, 1514, or 1515.1So in original. Probably should be followed by a comma.1

children.label: F
children.text: A nonoriginating material of heading 1701 used or consumed in the production of a good of any of headings 1701 through 1703.

children.label: G
children.text: A nonoriginating material of chapter 17 or heading 1805 used in the production of a good of subheading 1806.10.

children.label: H
children.text: Nonoriginating peaches, pears, or apricots of chapter 8 or 20, used in the production of a good of heading 2008.

children.label: i
children.text: subheading 2009.90, or tariff item 2106.90.54 (concentrated mixtures of fruit or vegetable juice, fortified with minerals or vitamins); or

children.label: ii
children.text: tariff item 2202.99.37 (mixtures of fruit or vegetable juices, fortified with minerals or vitamins).

children.label: J
children.text: A nonoriginating material of any of headings 2203 through 2208 used or consumed in the production of a good provided for under heading 2207 or 2208.

children.heading: Goods provided for under chapters 1 through 27
children.label: 3 Goods provided for under chapters 1 through 27
children.text: Paragraph (1) does not apply to a nonoriginating material used or consumed in the production of a good provided for in chapters 1 through 27 unless the nonoriginating material is provided for in a different subheading than the subheading of the good for which origin is being determined.

children.heading: Goods classified under chapters 50 through 60
children.label: A Goods classified under chapters 50 through 60
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (C), a textile or apparel good provided for in any of chapters 50 through 60 or heading 9619 that is not an originating good because certain nonoriginating materials used in the production of the good do not undergo an applicable change in tariff classification set forth in Annex 4–B of the USMCA, shall be considered to be an originating good if the total weight of all such materials, including elastomeric yarns, is not more than 10 percent of the total weight of the good and the good meets all other applicable requirements of this section.

children.heading: Goods classified under chapters 61 through 63
children.label: B Goods classified under chapters 61 through 63
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (C), a textile or apparel good provided for in chapter 61, 62, or 63 that is not an originating good because certain fibers or yarns used in the production of the component of the good that determines the tariff classification of the good do not undergo an applicable change in tariff classification set forth in Annex 4–B of the USMCA shall be considered to be an originating good if the total weight of all such fibers or yarns in the component, including elastomeric yarns, is not more than 10 percent of the total weight of the component and the good meets all other applicable requirements of this section.

children.heading: Goods containing nonoriginating elastomeric yarns
children.label: C Goods containing nonoriginating elastomeric yarns

children.heading: Goods classified under chapters 50 through 60 or heading 9619
children.label: i Goods classified under chapters 50 through 60 or heading 9619
children.text: A textile or apparel good described in subparagraph (A) containing nonoriginating elastomeric yarns shall be considered to be an originating good only if the nonoriginating elastomeric yarns contained in the good do not exceed 7 percent of the total weight of the good.

children.heading: Goods classified under chapters 61 through 63
children.label: ii Goods classified under chapters 61 through 63
children.text: A textile or apparel good described in subparagraph (B) containing nonoriginating elastomeric yarns shall be considered to be an originating good only if the nonoriginating elastomeric yarns contained in the component of the good that determines the tariff classification of the good do not exceed 7 percent of the total weight of the good.

children.heading: Fungible materials used in production
children.label: 1 Fungible materials used in production
children.text: Subject to paragraph (3), if originating and nonoriginating fungible materials are used or consumed in the production of a good, the determination of whether the materials are originating may be made on the basis of any of the inventory management methods set forth in regulations implementing this section.

children.heading: Fungible goods commingled and exported
children.label: 2 Fungible goods commingled and exported
children.text: Subject to paragraph (3), if originating and nonoriginating fungible goods are commingled and exported in the same form, the determination of whether the goods are originating may be made on the basis of any of the inventory management methods set forth in regulations implementing this section.

children.heading: Use of inventory management method
children.label: 3 Use of inventory management method
children.text: A person that selects an inventory management method for purposes of paragraph (1) or (2) shall use that inventory management method throughout the fiscal year of the person.

children.heading: Accessories, spare parts, tools, and instructional or other information materials
children.label: h Accessories, spare parts, tools, and instructional or other information materials
A
children.text: be treated as originating if the good is an originating good;

children.label: B
children.text: be disregarded in determining whether a good is a good wholly obtained or produced entirely in the territory of one or more USMCA countries or satisfies a process or change in tariff classification set forth in Annex 4–B of the USMCA; and

children.label: C
children.text: be taken into account as originating or nonoriginating materials, as the case may be, in calculating any applicable regional value content of the good set forth in Annex 4–B of the USMCA.

children.label: A
children.text: the accessories, spare parts, tools, or instructional or other information materials are classified with and delivered with, but not invoiced separately from, the good; and

children.label: B
children.text: the types, quantities, and value of the accessories, spare parts, tools, or instructional or other information materials are customary for the good.

children.heading: Packaging materials and containers for retail sale
children.label: i Packaging materials and containers for retail sale
children.text: Packaging materials and containers in which a good is packaged for retail sale, if classified with the good, shall be disregarded in determining whether all of the nonoriginating materials used in the production of the good undergo the applicable process or change in tariff classification requirement set forth in Annex 4–B of the USMCA, or whether the good is a good wholly obtained or produced entirely in the territory of one or more USMCA countries. If the good is subject to a regional value content requirement set forth in that Annex, the value of such packaging materials and containers shall be taken into account as originating or nonoriginating materials, as the case may be, in calculating the regional value content of the good.

children.heading: Packing materials and containers for shipment
children.label: j Packing materials and containers for shipment
children.text: Packing materials and containers for shipment shall be disregarded in determining whether a good is an originating good.

children.heading: Indirect materials
children.label: k Indirect materials
children.text: An indirect material shall be treated as an originating material without regard to where it is produced.

children.label: A
children.text: unloading, reloading, separation from a bulk shipment, storing, labeling, or marking, as required by a USMCA country; or

children.label: B
children.text: any other operation necessary to preserve the good in good condition or to transport the good to the territory of the importing USMCA country; or
does not remain under the control of customs authorities in a country other than a USMCA country.

children.heading: Goods classifiable as goods put up in sets
children.label: m Goods classifiable as goods put up in sets

children.heading: Goods other than textile or apparel goods
children.label: 1 Goods other than textile or apparel goods
A
children.text: each of the goods in the set is an originating good; or

children.label: B
children.text: the total value of the nonoriginating goods in the set does not exceed 10 percent of the value of the set.

children.label: A
children.text: each of the goods in the set is an originating good; or

children.label: B
children.text: the total value of the nonoriginating goods in the set does not exceed 10 percent of the value of the set.
mere dilution with water or another substance that does not materially alter the characteristics of the good; or
any production or pricing practice with respect to which it may be demonstrated, by a preponderance of the evidence, that the object of the practice was to circumvent this section.

children.label: A
children.text: take effect on the date on which the USMCA enters into force; and

children.label: B
children.text: apply with respect to a good entered for consumption, or withdrawn from warehouse for consumption, on or after that date.

children.heading: Transition from NAFTA treatment
children.label: 2 Transition from NAFTA treatment
children.text: Section 202 of the North American Free Trade Agreement Implementation Act (), as in effect on the day before the date on which the USMCA enters into force, shall continue to apply on and after that date with respect to a good entered for consumption, or withdrawn from warehouse for consumption, before that date.19 U.S.C. 33322See References in Text note below.2
